channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden to award medals of honor, valor in waning days at White House
item.description: President Biden will award the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers and the Medal of Valor to eight public safety officers at the White House on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ski Accident On New Hampshire Mountain claims life of 12-year-old boy
item.description: A tragic New Year's Eve skiing accident claimed the life of a 12-year-old boy who became unconscious at Cranmore mountain in New Hampshire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal courts will not refer allegations that Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas may have violated ethics laws to the Justice Department, the judiciary's policymaking body said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Help sought locating boy, 14, who disappeared in Los Angeles County
item.description: Authorities asked the public Thursday for help locating a 14-year-old boy who disappeared in the city of Maywood on New Year's Day. Anthony George Martinez was last seen around 1:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Cybertruck explosion marks second high-profile incident at Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: The Cybertruck fire and explosion was preceded by a 2021 bomb threat that was meant to serve as a message to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, WGN 9

item.title: An Army veteran's path to radicalization followed divorces, struggling businesses in Texas
item.description: BEAUMONT, Texas (AP) — Shamsud-Din Jabbar grew up in Texas, joined the U.S. Army and eventually settled in Houston, where he spun up a real estate business and made $120,000 a year for one of the world’s largest consulting firms. But the 42-year-old U.S.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Investigators say New Orleans attacker acted alone
item.description: Top law enforcement officials are briefing President Joe Biden and Congress on two deadly New Year's Day attacks, one in New Orleans and the other at President-elect Donald Trump's hotel in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Jessi Turnure, KTLA 5

item.title: Governor seeks resignation of Western New Mexico University board after spending revelations
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — New Mexico Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham has asked for the immediate resignation of regents at Western New Mexico State University after revelations of wasteful spending and failures in financial oversight, according to documents released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inside the military career of Shamsud-Din Jabbar, from Afghanistan deployment to Staff Sergeant
item.description: Shamsud-Din Bahar Jabbar, a former Army Staff Sergeant, had a distinguished military career from 2007 to 2020, serving as an IT and Human Resources Specialist with deployments in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, Jason Nguyen, KPRC 2

item.title: What we know about the New Year's Day attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: John Capodice, actor who appeared in 'Ace Ventura,' 'Seinfeld' and more, dies at 83
item.description: News of Capodice’s death on Monday was publicly announced in an obituary posted by the Pizzi Funeral Home in Northvale, New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames 'open borders' for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump blamed the situation at the U.S.-southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Joe Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Jews around the globe felt 'tipping point' in hate in 2024
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver ID'd as Army soldier from Colorado: Source
item.description: Livelsberger allegedly served at one point at the same military base as the man suspected of driving into a crowd in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, NewsNation

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: The FBI thwarted an apparent plot to harm employees at a Florida office of the American Israel Public Affairs Committee.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Meet the Danish-Swedish farmdog, the newest breed in the American Kennel Club's lineup
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Say hello to the latest dog in the American Kennel Club's lineup of recognized breeds. Or you might say “hej.” The Danish-Swedish farmdog — yep, that's the official name — joined the pack Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the victims in New Orleans truck attack
item.description: Authorities are yet to release the names of the 15 victims from the New Year’s Day truck attack on Bourbon Street, but families have started sharing tributes.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, Gage Goulding, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Multiple dead, injured after car plowed into crowd on Bourbon Street in act of terrorism
item.description: Multiple people are dead after the New Orleans Police Department reports a car may have plowed into a group of people on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, WGN 9

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Death toll in New Orleans attack rises to 15, coroner says; more details emerge as investigation continues
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 15 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New Orleans New Year's Day ramming is the latest attack to use a vehicle as a deadly weapon
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police say a man who drove a pickup truck at high speed into a crowd of people celebrating the new year in New Orleans early Wednesday was hell-bent on creating carnage.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James' youngest son Bryce commits to Arizona
item.description: Bryce James, son of NBA star LeBron James, is heading to Arizona. Bryce James announced his decision Wednesday on Instagram and his father had a quick response.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, Fox 5

item.title: New York employers must now offer paid medical leave during pregnancy
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Pregnant New Yorkers will be entitled to at least 20 hours of paid leave to attend prenatal medical appointments under a law that took effect Wednesday. Gov. Kathy Hochul said the policy makes New York the first state in the country to offer paid leave for prenatal care.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harry Chandler, Navy medic who survived Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor, dies at 103
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Harry Chandler, a Navy medic who helped pull injured sailors from the oily waters of Pearl Harbor after the 1941 Japanese attack on the naval base, has died. He was 103.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter sought to expand democracy worldwide long after he left the White House
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Amid everything else on his desk — the Iran hostage crisis, domestic economic turmoil, the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan and a grueling 1980 reelection fight — President Jimmy Carter elevated the independence of a country in southern Africa as a top agenda item.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4
